untranslatable_reason: This signal requires Elliott Wave pattern recognition (5-wave main trend + 3-wave correction), Fibonacci sequence wave length measurement in days, and manual trendline drawing. These are qualitative pattern analysis techniques that cannot be expressed using only the available quantitative indicators (RSI, MACD, ATR, moving averages, etc.). The signal demands: (1) identification of specific wave structures by count and form, (2) measurement of wave durations against Fibonacci numbers, (3) calculation of reaction ratios against Fibonacci levels, and (4) manual trendline interpretation. None of these capabilities exist in the allowed indicator set.
translation_notes: Elliott Wave analysis is a subjective pattern recognition methodology requiring visual wave counting and manual trendline placement. While trendline breaks could theoretically be operationalized with price action rules (e.g., close below sma_20 or ADX crossovers), the core logic—identifying 5-3 wave patterns and measuring wave lengths in days against Fibonacci sequences—cannot be mechanized without specialized Elliott Wave detection indicators not in the allowed set. Recommend: (1) implementing a custom Elliott Wave detector, (2) using ADX_14 > 25 for trend confirmation, or (3) substituting with Fibonacci retracement levels (e.g., price touches sma_50 near 0.618 Fibonacci level) if directional context is available.
